title: Sebi proposes shifting ODR oversight to MIIs for quicker grievance redressal
summary: Sebi proposes shifting online dispute resolution administration to market infrastructure institutions. This move aims to significantly speed up investor grievance redressal processes. Market infrastructure institutions will now handle conciliator and arbitrator appointments and oversight. Investors will have a voice in arbitrator selection, while conciliators are appointed directly. This framework change is estimated to shorten the overall resolution process by twenty-one days.

summary: We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources

title: Dividend alert! Last day to buy Wipro, DLF, Persistent Systems among 12 stocks for dividend rewards. Do you own any?
summary: IT major Wipro earlier this month announced an interim dividend of Rs 2 per equity share along with its Q1 results, with the record date being fixed on July 27. The company said its board of directors considered and approved a dividend of Rs 2 per equity share (face value Rs 2 each) during its meeting, which will be paid on or before August 14 this year.

title: Banking liquidity hits lowest since June 29; RBI data reveals Rs 2,884 crore deficit
summary: Mumbai banking system liquidity has fallen to its lowest point since late June.  This deficit is attributed to increased credit growth and central bank foreign exchange market actions.  System liquidity experienced a deficit of ₹2,884 crore on Wednesday.  The Reserve Bank of India's intervention is expected to worsen this liquidity gap.  Economists anticipate further deficits in the coming days due to these factors.

title: NSE cash market turnover hits 23-month high in June
summary: Indian equity markets experienced robust trading in June, reaching a 23-month high in cash market turnover. Proprietary traders' share of cash market turnover decreased, indicating broader investor participation. Foreign investors significantly increased their presence in equity futures, reaching a record high. Domestic institutional investors also boosted their share in equity futures during this period. Individual investors raised their participation in equity options, showing growing retail trader involvement.

title: Financials dominate Nifty50 in June, IT sector weight hits 23-year low
summary: Financials increased their Nifty 50 weight to a four-month high in June. This rise followed sector outperformance and renewed foreign investor inflows. Information Technology continued its decline, losing significant weight in the index. The IT sector's weight is near a 23-year low due to global spending concerns. Weak global technology spending and AI disruption also impacted the IT sector.

title: Smaller stocks gain ground as largecaps lose market share
summary: Large-cap stocks have seen their market share decline over six years. Mid, small, and micro-cap stocks have gained significant ground during this period. The Nifty's share of total market capitalization fell to a multi-year low. Meanwhile, the Nifty Smallcap 250 reached a multi-year high. This trend highlights the increasing weight of smaller companies in the market.

title: D-St set for a negative opening as GIFT Nifty signals weak start
summary: Domestic equities extended losses for a fourth consecutive session, with the Nifty declining. Investor sentiment remained fragile amid a sharp surge in Brent crude oil prices. Foreign portfolio investors net sold shares worth Rs 2,999 crore on Wednesday. The rupee traded in a narrow range, appreciating marginally by around 0.05%. Analysts suggest Indian equities are likely to remain under pressure in the near term.

title: Stocks in news: Infosys, Cipla, Meesho, Suzlon Energy, IndiGo, NTPC
summary: Markets extended their losing streak for a fourth consecutive session. Infosys reported a 12% year-on-year profit growth in its first quarter. Cipla's net profit fell 39% due to weakness in its North America business. IndiGo reported a loss of Rs 238 crore in its first quarter. Several other companies will announce their first quarter results today.

title: Sebi introduces fast-track process for securities transmission
summary: Sebi has revised the securities transmission framework for investors.  A fast-track mechanism is introduced for small-value claims.  Documentation requirements are simplified, and probate of a will is removed.  Claims up to ₹10 lakh in physical and ₹30 lakh in demat form are covered.  This aims for a more efficient and investor-friendly process.

title: Private bank ETFs back in focus as valuations turn attractive
summary: Private bank stocks present a thematic investment opportunity with reasonable valuations.  These banks are showing improved margins and strong credit growth potential.  Analysts believe margin pressures are now behind the banking sector.  Credit growth is expected to accelerate significantly over the next two years.  Investors can consider accumulating private banks with a medium-term outlook.

title: North India leads India's expanding stock market investor base
summary: North India now leads the nation in registered investors, surpassing the West. This significant shift accelerated after the pandemic with increased internet access. The North region saw its investor base expand six times since FY19. Maharashtra remains the largest state, though its share has declined. Uttar Pradesh has now overtaken Gujarat as the second-largest investor state.

title: Sebi plans a wider investment play for portfolio managers
summary: The regulator said the review has been done due the rapid expansion of the PMS industry, where AUM (assets under management) has multiplied to ₹42.61 lakh crore as of May 2026 from ₹18.07 lakh crore in April 2019. "Considering the increasing sophistication of investors, growing demand for more personalised solutions and diverse investment portfolio, a need was felt to review the PM (portfolio manager) Regulations," Sebi said in a discussion paper on Thursday.

title: Regulator warns insurers against deep discounts on fire risk cover
summary: The insurance regulator has flagged aggressive underpricing in fire insurance, with discounts of up to 99% on large industrial risks, warning it could hurt insurers’ financial health and underwriting discipline. Fire insurance premiums fell sharply in Q1 FY27 to ₹8,087 crore from ₹11,206 crore a year ago, after strong growth in FY26.

title: Fintechs tell NPCI its 'default app' plan could kill competition
summary: Seven Indian fintech firms have written to NPCI regarding its UPI Meta protocol. They argue the proposed framework risks locking customers into a single default payment app. This change could adversely impact competition and innovation within the digital payments ecosystem. The companies request broader consultation before NPCI proceeds with the framework. They believe the current UPI checkout flow does not require such a significant alteration.

title: Godrej Capital aims to grow gold loan book to Rs 5000 crores by 2031
summary: Godrej Capital aims to build a Rs 5,000 crore gold loan book.  This expansion follows their acquisition of Kanakadurga Finance's gold loan portfolio.  The company plans to increase its specialized gold loan branches to 350.  This strategy involves deepening presence in existing regions and launching new operations.  Godrej Capital targets a Rs 1 lakh crore overall AUM by 2031.

title: Three US law firms probe HDFC Bank over alleged Maharashtra deposit payments
summary: HDFC Bank is facing fresh scrutiny in the US after three law firms launched investigations into whether the lender violated federal securities laws over allegations that it disguised ₹45 crore in payments to the Maharashtra State Road Development Corporation (MSRDC) as marketing expenses.

title: Ujjivan Small Finance Bank grows share of secured loan to over 50%
summary: Ujjivan Small Finance Bank has achieved a significant milestone by increasing its secured loan portfolio. This strategic shift now represents over fifty percent of the bank's total loan book. The bank's gross loan book expanded by twenty-nine percent year-on-year. Ujjivan reported a three-fold jump in its first quarter net profit. Provisions were forty-three percent lower, aiding the profit growth.

title: Nuclear fusion startups seek regulatory clarity, more patient capital
summary: One of the key recommendations from fusion startups is to create a separate regulatory framework for fusion reactors, arguing that the current law largely applies rules designed for conventional fission reactors

title: Consulting partner pool soars, taps academia, policy and tech too
summary: Consulting and Big Four firms in India have more than doubled their partner ranks over last three to five years amid rising demand for specialized expertise. They are also hiring beyond traditional consulting into tech, academia, and startups as AI reshapes client needs.

title: ITR filing deadline 2026: 5 income tax penalties every taxpayer must know for AY 2026-27
summary: Five income tax penalties applicable for AY 2026-27 include late Income Tax Return filing fees, revised return charges and misreporting penalties. Here's why filing your ITR before 31 helps avoid fines, delays and tax notices.
